findings.title: Installed on-the-fly via a package runner
findings.detail: Server 'hex-ssh' is fetched and executed at launch via 'npx' with auto-accept flag(s) -y; package spec: @levnikolaevich/hex-ssh-mcp.
findings.recommendation: Install the server as a pinned dependency and invoke the local binary, or vendor it. If a runner is unavoidable, pin an exact version (see unpinned_source).

findings.title: Package source is unpinned or mutable
findings.detail: Server 'hex-ssh' package spec '@levnikolaevich/hex-ssh-mcp' is unpinned (pin strength: none): no version specified (resolves to whatever the registry serves now).
findings.recommendation: Pin an exact version (pkg@1.2.3) or a git commit SHA. Avoid @latest and branch refs.

candidate_capabilities.title: Filesystem read

evidence.snippet: Read remote file with hash-annotated lines. Us

candidate_capabilities.rationale: Reading file contents is the most common SOURCE half of an exfiltration chain — pair it with any network_egress tool and the server can read a secret/config/document and send it out. Modelling read as a capability is what lets the read-then-send toxic combination fire.

data_profile.note: Rating reflects high-confidence matches; credentials_secrets, source_code matched only in prose (medium confidence) and would raise the rating if confirmed in review.

toxic_combinations.title: Read-then-send exfiltration chain
toxic_combinations.detail: The server can both access secrets/credentials and make outbound network calls — a path to exfiltrate them to an attacker-chosen host.

toxic_combinations.title: Code execution alongside secret access
toxic_combinations.detail: Arbitrary execution combined with credential access means a single tool call can read and abuse every secret the server holds.

toxic_combinations.title: Arbitrary file read paired with egress
toxic_combinations.detail: The server can read file contents and make outbound network calls — a read-then-send exfiltration path. The read accepts an arbitrary path (or the server is scoped to a broad directory), so it can reach secrets/configs far beyond the task.
